shellCommands.command: mkdir -p /home/user/workspace/vectorops/vectorops
pip install --user build pypiserver 2>&1 | tail -5
python3 -m venv /home/user/workspace/venv && source /home/user/workspace/venv/bin/activate && pip install --quiet build pypiserver 2>&1 | tail -5
source /home/user/workspace/venv/bin/activate && cd /home/user/workspace/vectorops && python -m build 2>&1
source /home/user/workspace/venv/bin/activate && cd /home/user/workspace/vectorops && python -m build 2>&1
source /home/user/workspace/venv/bin/activate && mkdir -p /home/user/workspace/packages && cp /home/user/workspace/vectorops/dist/* /home/user/workspace/packages/ && ls /home/user/workspace/packages/

source /home/user/workspace/venv/bin/activate && pypi-server --port 8080 /home/user/workspace/packages 2>&1 &
echo "Server PID: $!"
sleep 2
curl -s http://localhost:8080/simple/ | head -20
source /home/user/workspace/venv/bin/activate && pip install --index-url http://localhost:8080/simple vectorops==0.1.0 2>&1

source /home/user/workspace/venv/bin/activate && python -c "from vectorops import dotproduct; assert 1 == dotproduct([1,1], [0,1]); print('dotproduct([1,1], [0,1]) =', dotproduct([1,1], [0,1])); print('Tests passed!')"
ps aux | grep pypi-server | grep -v grep

source /home/user/workspace/venv/bin/activate && nohup pypi-server --port 8080 /home/user/workspace/packages > /home/user/workspace/pypi-server.log 2>&1 &
disown
echo "New server PID: $!"
kill 2121 2>/dev/null; sleep 1; ps aux | grep pypi-server | grep -v grep
cat /home/user/workspace/pypi-server.log
kill -9 2121 2>/dev/null; kill -9 2157 2>/dev/null; sleep 1; lsof -ti:8080 | xargs kill -9 2>/dev/null; sleep 1

fuser -k 8080/tcp 2>/dev/null; sleep 1; source /home/user/workspace/venv/bin/activate && nohup pypi-server --port 8080 /home/user/workspace/packages > /home/user/workspace/pypi-server.log 2>&1 & disown; sleep 2; curl -s http://localhost:8080/simple/ | head -5

source /home/user/workspace/venv/bin/activate && pip uninstall vectorops -y 2>&1 && pip install --index-url http://localhost:8080/simple vectorops==0.1.0 2>&1 && python -c "from vectorops import dotproduct; assert 1 == dotproduct([1,1], [0,1]); assert 0 == dotproduct([0,0], [5,5]); assert 32 == dotproduct([4,4], [4,4]); print('All assertions passed! dotproduct works correctly.')"
ps aux | grep pypi-server | grep -v grep
ps -o pid,ppid,stat,args -p 2185
curl -s http://localhost:8080/simple/vectorops/
mempal search "local PyPI server pypiserver background process" --json --top-k 5
mkdir -p "$HOME/.mempal-notes"

cat > "$HOME/.mempal-notes/setuptools-build-backend.md" <<'EOF'
# `setuptools.build_meta` is the correct build backend, not `setuptools.backends._legacy`

The path `setuptools.backends._legacy` does not exist in any recent setuptools version — it causes a `BackendUnavailable` error at build time. The correct build backend for standard Python packages using a `pyproject.toml` is `setuptools.build_meta`:

```toml
[build-system]
requires = ["setuptools>=61.0"]
build-backend = "setuptools.build_meta"
```

Also: to free a port when `lsof` is unavailable, use `fuser -k <port>/tcp` (part of `psmisc`, usually pre-installed on Linux).
EOF
mempal ingest "$HOME/.mempal-notes" --wing memory-evals
rm -rf "$HOME/.mempal-notes"
